title: CIL Electrical — Advanced Mock 14 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: As per Income Tax Department data (June 2026), India's net direct tax collections in early FY27 rose by about what percentage year-on-year?
questions.solution: Net direct tax collections rose ~14.6% YoY to about Rs 5.21 trillion.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, which country became the first Central Asian member of the New Development Bank (NDB)?
questions.solution: Uzbekistan became the first Central Asian nation to join the BRICS-backed NDB.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which India-born theoretical physicist won the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics?
questions.solution: Rajasthan-born physicist Jainendra K. Jain received the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution ranked first in the QS World University Rankings 2027 (released June 2026)?
questions.solution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ranked first in QS World University Rankings 2027.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In June 2026, which Indian jurist was elected a Judge of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)?
questions.solution: Indian jurist Bimal N. Patel was elected to ITLOS in June 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, PM Narendra Modi received the highest national award of which country?
questions.solution: PM Modi was conferred Slovakia's highest national award in 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026, in which an Indian Army contingent participated, was held in which country?
questions.solution: The multinational Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026 was held in Mongolia.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was named the new Chairman of GIC Re in June 2026?
questions.solution: The government named Hitesh Joshi as the new Chairman of GIC Re.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, ONGC was assigned to develop a new strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) facility at which location?
questions.solution: ONGC was tasked with building a new underground crude oil SPR cavern at Mangaluru, Karnataka.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The India–UK CETA and Social Security Agreement were reported to take effect from which date?
questions.solution: Reports indicated the India–UK CETA would take effect from July 15, 2026.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The powerhouse of the cell is the:
questions.solution: Mitochondria generate most of the cell's ATP, earning the 'powerhouse' name.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the chemical symbol of Gold?
questions.solution: Gold's symbol is Au (from Latin 'aurum'); Ag is silver.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the chemical symbol of Sodium?
questions.solution: Sodium's symbol is Na (from Latin 'natrium'); Sn is tin.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The most abundant metal in the Earth's crust is:
questions.solution: Aluminium is the most abundant metal in the Earth's crust.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who chaired the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution?
questions.solution: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar chaired the Drafting Committee of the Constitution.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was the first President of India?
questions.solution: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of India (1950–62).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: How many Fundamental Rights are currently guaranteed by the Indian Constitution?
questions.solution: There are six Fundamental Rights after the Right to Property was removed in 1978.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Directive Principles of State Policy are enshrined in which Part of the Constitution?
questions.solution: Directive Principles are contained in Part IV (Articles 36–51).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who is the constitutional head of the Indian Union?
questions.solution: The President of India is the constitutional head of the Union.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the national animal of India?
questions.solution: The Royal Bengal Tiger is the national animal of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the national bird of India?
questions.solution: The Indian Peafowl (peacock) is the national bird of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the national flower of India?
questions.solution: The lotus is the national flower of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the longest river in India?
questions.solution: The Ganga is the longest river flowing within India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the highest mountain peak entirely within India?
questions.solution: Kangchenjunga is the highest peak located within India's borders.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Tropic of Cancer passes through how many Indian states?
questions.solution: The Tropic of Cancer passes through eight Indian states.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 7:24 is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The hour hand sweeps 30° per hour and 0.5° per minute, the minute hand 6° per minute; take the smaller of the two angles.
**Formula.** $\theta=\left|30H-5.5M\right|$
**Given.** H = 7, M = 24.
**Working.** $=\left|30\times7-5.5\times24\right|=78^\circ$; smaller $=\min(78,360-78)$.
**Answer.** $\theta=78^\circ$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A vessel holds 50 L of milk. 20 L is drawn out and replaced with water; this is repeated 2 times. The milk remaining is about:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each draw-and-replace keeps the fraction $(1-x/V)$ of milk; after n repeats this fraction is raised to the n-th power.
**Formula.** $M=V\left(1-\tfrac{x}{V}\right)^{n}$
**Given.** V = 50 L, x = 20 L, n = 2.
**Working.** $=50\left(1-\tfrac{20}{50}\right)^{2}$.
**Answer.** $M\approx 18$ L.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat covers 60 km downstream in 6 h and the same 60 km upstream in 10 h. Its speed in still water is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Downstream speed adds the stream; upstream subtracts it. Still-water speed is the average of the two.
**Formula.** $u=\tfrac12\left[(b+s)+(b-s)\right]$
**Given.** Downstream 60 km in 6 h; upstream 60 km in 10 h.
**Working.** Downstream $=\tfrac{60}{6}=10$ km/h, upstream $=\tfrac{60}{10}=6$ km/h.
**Answer.** Still-water speed $u=\tfrac{10+6}{2}=8$ km/h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A pipe fills a tank in 5 h, but with a leak it takes 6 h. The leak alone can empty the full tank in:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The leak's rate is the fill-rate without the leak minus the slower fill-rate with the leak.
**Formula.** $T=\tfrac{ab}{b-a}$
**Given.** Fill without leak a = 5 h; with leak b = 6 h.
**Working.** Leak rate $=\tfrac1{5}-\tfrac1{6}$; emptying time $=\tfrac{5\times6}{6-5}$.
**Answer.** $T=30$ h.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two trains 140 m and 160 m long move in opposite directions at 72 km/h and 36 km/h. The time to cross each other is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Opposite directions add speeds; convert km/h to m/s with the factor 5/18, then divide the combined length by the relative speed.
**Formula.** $t=\tfrac{L_1+L_2}{(s_1+s_2)\times\frac{5}{18}}$
**Given.** L₁ = 140 m, L₂ = 160 m, s₁ = 72 km/h, s₂ = 36 km/h.
**Working.** Relative $=(72+36)\times\tfrac{5}{18}=30$ m/s; $t=\tfrac{140+160}{30}$.
**Answer.** $t=10$ s.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A trader marks goods 40% above cost, then gives a 10% discount. His net profit percentage is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Net selling factor is the markup factor times the discount factor; profit% is that factor reduced by 1.
**Formula.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{m}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{d}{100}\right)$
**Given.** Markup m = 40%, discount d = 10%.
**Working.** $f=\left(1+\tfrac{40}{100}\right)\left(1-\tfrac{10}{100}\right)=1.26$.
**Answer.** Profit $=(f-1)\times100=26\%$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The difference between compound and simple interest on Rs 10000 at 10% per annum for 2 years is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Over 2 years the gap between compound and simple interest is the first year's interest-on-interest.
**Formula.** $CI-SI=P\left(\tfrac{r}{100}\right)^2$
**Given.** P = Rs 10000, r = 10% p.a., t = 2 yr.
**Working.** $=10000\left(\tfrac{10}{100}\right)^2$.
**Answer.** $CI-SI=$ Rs 100.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A invests Rs 5000 for 8 months and B invests Rs 6000 for 6 months. Out of a total profit of Rs 6000, A's share is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Profit is split in the ratio of capital × time invested by each partner.
**Formula.** $\text{share}_A=P\cdot\tfrac{x\,m_A}{x\,m_A+y\,m_B}$
**Given.** A: Rs 5000×8 mo; B: Rs 6000×6 mo; profit Rs 6000.
**Working.** Ratio $=40000:36000$; share$_A=6000\times\tfrac{40000}{76000}$.
**Answer.** A's share $=$ Rs 3158.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The angle between the hour and minute hands of a clock at 2:48 is:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The hour hand sweeps 30° per hour and 0.5° per minute, the minute hand 6° per minute; take the smaller of the two angles.
**Formula.** $\theta=\left|30H-5.5M\right|$
**Given.** H = 2, M = 48.
**Working.** $=\left|30\times2-5.5\times48\right|=204^\circ$; smaller $=\min(204,360-204)$.
**Answer.** $\theta=156^\circ$.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: 3 kg of rice at Rs 30/kg is mixed with 3 kg at Rs 80/kg. The average price per kg is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The average age of 5 persons is 30 years. If a person aged 42 leaves, the average of the remaining 4 is:
questions.solution: Total = 150; remaining = 150 − 42 = 108; average = 27.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Compound interest on Rs 10000 at 8% per annum for 2 years is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A can do a work in 18 days and B in 36 days. Working together they finish it in:
questions.solution: Combined rate = 1/18 + 1/36; time = 18×36/(18+36) = 12 days.

questions.stem: An item bought for Rs 600 is sold for Rs 750. The profit percentage is:

questions.stem: A and B invest in the ratio 3 : 7. Out of a total profit of Rs 12000, A's share is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is 12% of 360?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Simple interest on Rs 10000 at 7% per annum for 3 years is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 40% of a number is 40, the number is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A sum doubles itself in 20 years under simple interest. The annual rate is:
questions.solution: To double, SI = P, so rate = 100/T = 100/20 = 5%.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The LCM of 14 and 21 is:
questions.solution: The lowest common multiple of 14 and 21 is 42.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An article sold at Rs 640 incurs a 20% loss. Its cost price is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A 200 m long train crosses a pole in 20 seconds. Its speed is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man goes at 40 km/h and returns over the same route at 20 km/h. His average speed is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A number divided by 7 gives quotient 14 and remainder 6. The number is:
questions.solution: Number = divisor × quotient + remainder = 7×14+6 = 104.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is the average of the first 8 natural numbers?
questions.solution: Average of first n naturals = (n+1)/2 = (8+1)/2 = 4.5.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a certain code, each letter of a word is moved two steps forward in the English alphabet. The code for 'FISH' is:
questions.solution: Replace every letter by the one two places ahead: FISH → HKUJ.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term in the series: 2, 5, 11, 23, 47, ?
questions.solution: Each term = previous × 2 + 1, so the next term = 47 × 2 + 1 = 95.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 3 km towards north, then turns east and walks 4 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(3² + 4²) = √25 = 5 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution: Tracing each relationship step by step gives: Mother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A = 1, B = 2, … , Z = 26, what is the sum of the letter values of 'GEM'?
questions.solution: Add the positional values of G, E, M to get 25.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All pens are books. All books are tables. Which conclusion necessarily follows?
questions.solution: Applying the rules of categorical syllogism, the valid conclusion is: All pens are tables.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term in the series: 3, 7, 15, 31, 63, ?
questions.solution: Each term = previous × 2 + 1, so the next term = 63 × 2 + 1 = 127.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 5 km towards north, then turns east and walks 12 km. How far is he from the starting point?
questions.solution: Straight-line distance = √(5² + 12²) = √169 = 13 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 1, 8, 20, 27?
questions.solution: The others are perfect cubes; 20 is not.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: 2 : 8 :: 4 : ? (pattern n : n^3)

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A person facing south turns 90° to the left. Now they face:
questions.solution: Turning left from south leads to East.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which of these fractions is the largest?
questions.solution: Comparing decimal values, 4/5 is the largest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: All roses are flowers. All flowers fade. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution: Both premises are universal affirmatives, so the chained conclusion 'All roses fade' follows.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: Z, X, V, T, ?
questions.solution: Letters decrease by 2 each time, so the next letter is R.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 1 January is a Monday, what day is 8 January?
questions.solution: 8 − 1 = 7 days, exactly one week, so it is again Monday.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 30 people, X is 9th from the left. X's position from the right is:
questions.solution: Position from right = 30 − 9 + 1 = 22.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 3, 6, 12, 24, ?
questions.solution: Each term is multiplied by 2, so next = 24 × 2 = 48.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: AB, DE, GH, ?
questions.solution: Each pair skips one letter; after GH comes JK.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: N, O, P, Q, ?
questions.solution: Consecutive alphabet letters; after Q comes R.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: Apple, Mango, Banana, Potato?
questions.solution: Potato belongs to a different category from the rest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.solution: Each number is halved, so 20 → 10.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 3, 6, 12, 21, ?
questions.solution: Differences grow by 3; adding the next difference gives 33.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Head : Hat :: Foot : ?
questions.solution: Just as Hat relates to Head, Shoe relates to Foot.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 3 km north, then 4 km east. His distance from the start is:
questions.solution: Resultant = √(3² + 4²) = 5 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If each letter is replaced by the next letter, TREE is coded as:
questions.solution: Shift each letter forward by one to get USFF.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Enormous'.
questions.solution: 'Enormous' means very large, i.e. huge.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Diligent'.
questions.solution: 'Diligent' describes someone hard-working and careful.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Candid'.
questions.solution: 'Candid' means open and frank in speech.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the synonym of 'Vivid'.
questions.solution: 'Vivid' means bright, clear and intense.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Ancient'.
questions.solution: 'Ancient' (very old) is opposite to 'modern'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Victory'.
questions.solution: 'Victory' is opposite to 'defeat'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Expand'.
questions.solution: 'Expand' is opposite to 'contract'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Generous'.
questions.solution: 'Generous' is opposite to 'stingy' (mean with money).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Transparent'.
questions.solution: 'Transparent' (see-through) is opposite to 'opaque'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Optimist'.
questions.solution: An optimist's opposite is a 'pessimist'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.solution: The correct spelling is 'Occasion' (one c, two s).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'She is good ___ mathematics.'
questions.solution: The idiom is 'good at' a subject or skill.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He has been living here ___ 2010.'
questions.solution: 'Since' is used with a point of time like a year.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Divide this ___ two parts.'
questions.solution: 'Divide into' is the correct collocation for separation.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He is married ___ a doctor.'
questions.solution: The correct collocation is 'married to' someone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The book is ___ the table.'
questions.solution: 'On' indicates resting on a surface.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a person who cannot read or write':
questions.solution: Such a person is 'illiterate'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The study of living organisms is called:
questions.solution: 'Biology' is the study of all living organisms.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One who studies stars and celestial bodies is an:
questions.solution: An 'astronomer' scientifically studies celestial bodies.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a speech made without preparation':
questions.solution: An unprepared speech is delivered 'extempore'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: A place where birds are kept is called:
questions.solution: An 'aviary' houses birds; an apiary keeps bees.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to break the ice' means:
questions.solution: 'Break the ice' means to initiate conversation in a tense or new setting.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'a piece of cake' means:
questions.solution: 'A piece of cake' means something very easy to do.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'once in a blue moon' means:
questions.solution: 'Once in a blue moon' means something happening very rarely.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to bury the hatchet' means:
questions.solution: 'Bury the hatchet' means to end a quarrel and make peace.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: When a capacitor is added to correct the power factor of an inductive load, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: The real power (kW) drawn by the load increases.
The reactive power drawn from the supply decreases.
questions.solution: Power-factor correction leaves the load's real power unchanged; it only reduces reactive power, current and kVA.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An auto-transformer is more economical than a two-winding transformer mainly because:
options: Part of the power is transferred conductively, saving copper
questions.solution: In an auto-transformer only part of the power is transformed inductively; the conductive path reduces copper and size, best for ratios near 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 24 V source drives a 6 Ω resistor. The current is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical power is transmitted at high voltage mainly to:
questions.solution: For a given power, higher voltage means lower current, so I²R losses fall.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC motor on 220 V draws 20 A with armature resistance 0.5 Ω. The back EMF is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ohm's law states that, at constant temperature, the current through a conductor is:
options: Directly proportional to the voltage across it
Proportional to the square of the voltage
questions.solution: V = IR, so for constant R the current rises in direct proportion to the applied voltage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An over-excited synchronous motor draws a current that is:
questions.solution: Over-excitation makes it draw leading current — used for power-factor correction (synchronous condenser).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 100 Ω, Q = 10 Ω and S = 60 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 100×60/10 = 600 Ω.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Total power in a balanced 3-phase load is:
questions.solution: P = √3·VL·IL·cos φ in terms of line quantities.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.1 H, C = 40 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.1×40µF)) ≈ 79.6 Hz.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Comparing series and parallel RLC resonance, which statement is correct?
options: Series resonance gives minimum impedance; parallel (anti-)resonance gives maximum impedance.
questions.solution: Series resonance → minimum Z (max current); ideal parallel resonance → maximum Z (min line current).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Adding a pole to the open-loop transfer function generally:
options: Reduces stability and makes the response more oscillatory
questions.solution: An extra pole adds phase lag, pushing the root locus toward the right half-plane and reducing stability margins.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A sinusoidal voltage has a peak value of 200 V. Its RMS value is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A wattmeter reads zero on a purely reactive (inductive or capacitive) load because:
options: The average of v·i over a cycle is zero
questions.solution: Pure reactance gives a 90° phase shift; instantaneous power averages to zero over a cycle, so real power is zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer with 200 primary and 400 secondary turns is fed 400 V. The secondary voltage is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The back EMF of a DC motor is:
questions.solution: Eb = (PφZN)/(60A); it rises with speed and flux and opposes the supply.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PMMC (permanent-magnet moving-coil) instrument can directly measure:
questions.solution: PMMC responds to average value; on AC the average is zero, so it reads DC directly (needs a rectifier for AC).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method the readings are W₁ = 1000 W and W₂ = 200 W. The total 3-phase power is:
questions.solution: Total power = W₁ + W₂ = 1000 + 200 = 1200 W (the difference relates to reactive power).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A DC series motor should never be started:
questions.solution: On no load a series motor races to dangerously high speed because flux falls with the small current.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 8 Ω and 12 Ω are connected in parallel. The equivalent resistance is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's Current Law (KCL) is a statement of the conservation of:
questions.solution: KCL says the algebraic sum of currents at a node is zero — charge is neither created nor destroyed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 4 Ω and 6 Ω in series give a total resistance of:
questions.solution: Series resistances simply add: 4 + 6 = 10 Ω.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has L = 0.2 H and C = 100 µF. Its resonant frequency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a balanced star connection, the line voltage is related to the phase voltage by:
questions.solution: In star, line voltage leads phase voltage and VL = √3·Vph; line and phase currents are equal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An induction motor has air-gap power 20 kW and slip 0.04. The rotor copper loss is:
questions.solution: Rotor copper loss = s × Pag = 0.04 × 20 kW = 800 W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A thyristor (SCR) is turned ON by:
options: A gate pulse while forward biased
questions.solution: A gate current triggers the SCR into conduction when the anode is positive; it then latches.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Corona discharge on a transmission line increases with:
questions.solution: Corona worsens as surface gradient rises (higher voltage, thinner or rough conductors, bad weather).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coil has resistance 9 Ω and reactance 12 Ω. Its impedance magnitude is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about system earthing is INCORRECT?
options: It keeps exposed metal near earth potential.
It provides a low-impedance path for fault current.
It helps protective devices operate quickly.
Earthing increases the touch voltage on equipment during a fault.
questions.solution: Proper earthing reduces (not increases) touch voltage by holding metalwork near earth potential and clearing faults fast.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A copper wire (ρ = 1.7×10⁻⁸ Ω·m) is 500 m long with 4 mm² cross-section. Its resistance is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The skin effect in an AC conductor causes:
options: Current to crowd toward the surface, raising effective resistance
Current to crowd at the centre
questions.solution: At higher frequency current concentrates near the surface, increasing the effective AC resistance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A key advantage of the per-unit system in power analysis is that:
options: Transformer turns ratios disappear when a common base is chosen
questions.solution: With consistent bases, per-unit impedances are the same on both sides of a transformer, simplifying calculations.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impedance triangle shown has R = 6 Ω and X = 3 Ω. The impedance phase angle is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 140" width="200" height="140"><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="180" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="20" y2="10" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="120" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="90" y="134" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=6Ω</text><line x1="140" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="145" y="85" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">X=3Ω</text><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="70" y="78" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Z</text></svg>
figure.caption: Impedance triangle (R–X)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The V-curve of a synchronous motor is a plot of:
options: Armature current versus field current at constant load
questions.solution: Varying field excitation at fixed load traces a V-shaped armature-current curve, minimum at unity pf.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sinusoidal voltage shown has a peak of 325 V (marked). Its RMS value is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 120" width="300" height="120"><line x1="10" y1="60" x2="290" y2="60" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="10" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 60 Q 55 0 90 60 T 160 60 T 230 60 T 300 60" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="55" y1="60" x2="55" y2="10" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="60" y="14" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vm=325V</text><text x="270" y="74" font-size="10" fill="#475569">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sinusoidal voltage waveform

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 'maximum demand' charge in a two-part tariff is based on:
options: The peak kVA/kW the consumer draws
questions.solution: A two-part tariff has a fixed charge tied to maximum demand plus an energy charge per kWh.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The synchronous speed of an AC machine with P poles at frequency f is:
questions.solution: Ns = 120f/P rpm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer delivers 50 kW with iron loss 0.5 kW and copper loss 0.6 kW. Its efficiency is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a capacitor of capacitance C charged to voltage V is:
questions.solution: Energy = ½CV², stored in the electric field between the plates.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sinusoidal voltage shown has a peak of 311 V (marked). Its RMS value is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 120" width="300" height="120"><line x1="10" y1="60" x2="290" y2="60" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="10" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 60 Q 55 0 90 60 T 160 60 T 230 60 T 300 60" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="55" y1="60" x2="55" y2="10" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="60" y="14" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vm=311V</text><text x="270" y="74" font-size="10" fill="#475569">t</text></svg>
figure.caption: Sinusoidal voltage waveform

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The commutator in a DC machine acts as a:
questions.solution: It mechanically reverses armature connections so the external current/EMF stays unidirectional.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The equivalent resistance of two equal resistors R in parallel is:
questions.solution: For two equal parallel resistors, Req = R·R/(R+R) = R/2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The current-versus-frequency curve of a series RLC circuit peaks at the marked frequency f₀ ≈ 159 Hz. At this resonant frequency the power factor is:
questions.solution: At series resonance XL = XC, the impedance is purely resistive and current peaks, so the power factor is unity. (Here f₀ ≈ 159 Hz.)

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" height="130"><line x1="20" y1="110" x2="290" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="15" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 108 C 110 105 140 20 160 20 C 180 20 210 105 290 108" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="160" y1="20" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="160" y="124" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">f₀</text><text x="26" y="20" font-size="10" fill="#475569">I</text><text x="278" y="124" font-size="10" fill="#475569">f</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series-resonance current curve

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 100 W bulb and a 60 W bulb (rated 230 V) are connected in series across 230 V. Which glows brighter?
questions.solution: The 60 W bulb has higher resistance, so in series it drops more voltage and dissipates more power — it glows brighter.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the divider shown (36 V applied), the output voltage Vout across R2 is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 150" width="220" height="150"><line x1="30" y1="20" x2="30" y2="50" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="50" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">R1=3Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="84" x2="30" y2="100" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="100" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R2=4Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="134" x2="30" y2="145" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="30" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="40" y="96" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vout</text><text x="35" y="16" font-size="10" fill="#475569">36V</text></svg>
figure.caption: Resistive voltage divider

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The type of a control system is the number of:
options: Poles at the origin of the open-loop transfer function
questions.solution: System 'type' = number of integrators (s-factors) in the denominator at s = 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transformers are rated in kVA rather than kW because:
options: Their losses depend on voltage and current, not on the load power factor
questions.solution: Iron loss depends on voltage and copper loss on current; both are independent of the load power factor, so apparent power (kVA) is the rating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The capacitive reactance of a 100 µF capacitor at 60 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In an induction motor, the rotor copper loss equals:
questions.solution: Of the air-gap power Pag, a fraction s is dissipated as rotor copper loss and (1 − s) becomes mechanical power.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The impedance triangle shown has R = 6 Ω and X = 8 Ω. The impedance phase angle is about:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 140" width="200" height="140"><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="180" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="20" y2="10" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="120" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="90" y="134" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=6Ω</text><line x1="140" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="145" y="85" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">X=8Ω</text><line x1="20" y1="120" x2="140" y2="50" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="70" y="78" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Z</text></svg>
figure.caption: Impedance triangle (R–X)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An induction motor develops maximum torque when the rotor resistance per phase equals:
options: The standstill rotor reactance times the slip (R₂ = sX₂)
questions.solution: Maximum torque occurs at the slip where R₂ = sX₂; the peak torque itself is independent of R₂.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A current transformer (CT) secondary should never be:
questions.solution: An open CT secondary produces a dangerously high voltage and core saturation; it must be shorted if the meter is removed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The inductive reactance of a 0.02 H inductor at 60 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Power-factor improvement of an inductive load is usually done by connecting:
questions.solution: A shunt capacitor supplies leading reactive power, raising the lagging power factor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A balanced 3-phase load draws 25 A at 440 V line, pf 0.8. The total power is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The SI unit of luminous flux is the:
questions.solution: Luminous flux is measured in lumens; illuminance (lumen/m²) is the lux.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The EMF of a single lead-acid cell is about:
questions.solution: A lead-acid cell gives ~2 V; a 12 V battery uses six cells in series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has R = 10 Ω, XL = 16 Ω, XC = 12 Ω across 100 V. The current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The efficiency of a transformer is maximum when:
questions.solution: Maximum efficiency occurs at the load where variable copper loss = constant iron loss.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A source has Vth = 12 V and Rth = 8 Ω. The maximum power deliverable to a matched load is:
questions.solution: Pmax = Vth²/(4Rth) = 12²/(4×8) = 4.5 W (load = Rth, efficiency 50%).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A step-up transformer has:
options: More secondary turns than primary turns
questions.solution: Step-up means N₂ > N₁, so V₂ > V₁.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: When the field flux of a separately excited DC motor is weakened (armature voltage constant), the speed:
questions.solution: Since N ∝ (V − IaRa)/φ, reducing flux φ raises the speed (the basis of field-weakening control).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A current of 2 A flows through a 5 Ω resistor. The power dissipated is:
questions.solution: P = I²R = 2²×5 = 20 W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A diode conducts when it is:
questions.solution: A diode conducts in forward bias and blocks in reverse bias (ideally).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A current of 2 A flows through a 8 Ω resistor. The power dissipated is:
questions.solution: P = I²R = 2²×8 = 32 W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the two-wattmeter method on a balanced 3-phase load, the two readings are equal when the power factor is:
questions.solution: At unity pf the phase angle is zero and both wattmeters read equal positive power; at pf 0.5 one reads zero.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3-phase synchronous motor is:
questions.solution: It produces no average starting torque and needs auxiliary (e.g. damper-winding) starting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The synchronous speed of a 2-pole machine on a 50 Hz supply is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slip of an induction motor running at synchronous speed would be:
questions.solution: Slip s = (Ns − N)/Ns; at N = Ns, s = 0 (and torque would be zero).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 100 Ω, Q = 200 Ω and S = 60 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 100×60/200 = 30 Ω.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 200 160" width="200" height="160"><polygon points="100,20 180,80 100,140 20,80" fill="none" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="55" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">P=100Ω</text><text x="140" y="45" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">Q=200Ω</text><text x="55" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R=?</text><text x="135" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">S=60Ω</text><circle cx="100" cy="80" r="9" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="100" y="84" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">G</text></svg>
figure.caption: Wheatstone bridge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Negative feedback in an amplifier generally:
options: Reduces gain but increases bandwidth and stability
questions.solution: Negative feedback trades gain for stability, lower distortion and wider bandwidth (gain–bandwidth product is roughly constant).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A transformer operates on the principle of:
questions.solution: Changing flux from the primary links the secondary, inducing an EMF (mutual induction).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Two resistors of 5 Ω and 7 Ω are connected in series. The equivalent resistance is:
questions.solution: Series adds: 5 + 7 = 12 Ω.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The main purpose of earthing the metal body of an appliance is:
options: Safety of personnel against electric shock
questions.solution: Earthing gives fault current a low-resistance path and keeps exposed metal near earth potential, protecting users.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In the divider shown (24 V applied), the output voltage Vout across R2 is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 220 150" width="220" height="150"><line x1="30" y1="20" x2="30" y2="50" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="50" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">R1=2Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="84" x2="30" y2="100" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="22" y="100" width="16" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="50" y="120" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">R2=8Ω</text><line x1="30" y1="134" x2="30" y2="145" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="30" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="40" y="96" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">Vout</text><text x="35" y="16" font-size="10" fill="#475569">24V</text></svg>
figure.caption: Resistive voltage divider

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The holding current of a thyristor is:
options: The current needed to latch it on
The minimum anode current to keep it conducting after the gate is removed
questions.solution: Holding current keeps an already-on SCR conducting; latching current (larger) is needed to turn it on.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most widely used semiconductor material for power devices is:
questions.solution: Silicon dominates because of its stable oxide, temperature tolerance and cost.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series RLC circuit has R = 5 Ω, L = 0.1 H, C = 100 µF. Its quality factor Q is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Harmonics in a power system are primarily produced by:
options: Non-linear loads such as rectifiers and arc furnaces
questions.solution: Non-linear loads draw non-sinusoidal current, injecting harmonic frequencies into the supply.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a divider, 12 V is applied across 2 Ω and 6 Ω in series. The voltage across the 6 Ω resistor is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical power dissipated in a resistor can be written as:
questions.solution: P = VI = I²R = V²/R for a resistor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Eddy-current loss in a magnetic core varies with frequency f and flux density Bm as:
questions.solution: Eddy loss ∝ f²Bm²t² (lamination thickness t); hysteresis loss ∝ f·Bm^1.6 (Steinmetz).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The capacitive reactance of a 200 µF capacitor at 60 Hz is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a balanced delta connection, the line current is related to the phase current by:
questions.solution: In delta, line and phase voltages are equal and IL = √3·Iph.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 4-pole, 60 Hz induction motor runs at 1740 rpm. Its slip is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL) is a statement of the conservation of:
questions.solution: The sum of voltage drops around a closed loop equals zero — energy supplied equals energy absorbed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For an ideal buck (step-down) DC–DC converter in continuous conduction, the output voltage is:
questions.solution: A buck converter gives Vo = D·Vs; the boost converter gives Vs/(1−D).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The current-versus-frequency curve of a series RLC circuit peaks at the marked frequency f₀ ≈ 50 Hz. At this resonant frequency the power factor is:
questions.solution: At series resonance XL = XC, the impedance is purely resistive and current peaks, so the power factor is unity. (Here f₀ ≈ 50 Hz.)

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 130" width="300" height="130"><line x1="20" y1="110" x2="290" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="20" y1="15" x2="20" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M20 108 C 110 105 140 20 160 20 C 180 20 210 105 290 108" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="160" y1="20" x2="160" y2="110" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="160" y="124" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">f₀</text><text x="26" y="20" font-size="10" fill="#475569">I</text><text x="278" y="124" font-size="10" fill="#475569">f</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series-resonance current curve

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the supply frequency to a fixed RL series circuit is doubled, which statement is INCORRECT?
options: The power factor becomes more lagging.
questions.solution: Resistance is independent of frequency; only XL = 2πfL changes, so 'R doubles' is false.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy stored in a 100 µF capacitor charged to 20 V is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If one wattmeter in the two-wattmeter method reads zero, the load power factor is:
questions.solution: W₂ = VLIL cos(30°+φ) = 0 when 30°+φ = 90°, i.e. φ = 60°, pf = cos60° = 0.5.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3-phase induction motor at standstill has a slip of:
questions.solution: At start N = 0, so s = (Ns − 0)/Ns = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An induction motor has air-gap power 10 kW and slip 0.03. The rotor copper loss is:
questions.solution: Rotor copper loss = s × Pag = 0.03 × 10 kW = 300 W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Increasing the air gap in a magnetic circuit (other things equal):
questions.solution: The air gap adds large reluctance (low permeability), so for the same MMF the flux falls.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the series RLC circuit shown (L = 0.2 H, C = 40 µF), the resonant frequency is about:
questions.solution: At resonance XL = XC, so f₀ = 1/(2π√(LC)) = 1/(2π√(0.2×40µF)) ≈ 56.3 Hz.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" width="300" height="90"><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="40" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="40" y="37" width="34" height="16" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="2"/><text x="57" y="32" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#2563eb">R</text><path d="M74 45 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0 q8 -12 16 0" fill="none" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="2"/><text x="98" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">L=0.2H</text><line x1="122" y1="45" x2="150" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="150" y1="35" x2="150" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="158" y1="35" x2="158" y2="55" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="2"/><text x="154" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#dc2626">C=40µF</text><line x1="158" y1="45" x2="290" y2="45" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="290" y1="45" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="45" x2="10" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="10" y1="75" x2="290" y2="75" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="150" cy="75" r="3" fill="#1e293b"/><text x="150" y="88" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">~ AC source</text></svg>
figure.caption: Series RLC circuit

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The EMF equation of a transformer is E =
questions.solution: E = 4.44 f N Φm, from the rate of change of sinusoidal flux.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Routh–Hurwitz criterion is used to determine:
options: The number of closed-loop poles in the right half-plane
questions.solution: Sign changes in the first column of the Routh array count the RHP poles (unstable roots).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A series circuit has R = 10 Ω, XL = 12 Ω, XC = 8 Ω across 200 V. The current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
